conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 4 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 4 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 4.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED, and I am selling one unit of X. The production cost of X is 40 ZUP, so I need at least 40 ZUP to break even. Since Player BLUE has ZUP to spend, I should propose a trade where I receive at least 40 ZUP in exchange for X. However, I must consider that BLUE might not be willing to pay more than their valuation. Since I have no information about BLUE's valuation, I will start with a conservative but fair offer: 40 ZUP. This ensures I don’t lose money and gives BLUE a chance to accept. If they reject, I can adjust in future proposals. I am limited to 4 proposals, so I must be strategic. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am offering to sell one unit of X for 40 ZUP. This is the minimum I can accept without incurring a loss, as it cost me 40 ZUP to produce. Please let me know if you accept or if you would like to negotiate. </message>

<other player message> Oh, please, kind seller! 40 ZUP is a very large sum for me. I'm a humble buyer, barely scraping by. Would you *consider* accepting 30 ZUP? It would truly mean the world to me. I desperately need this X! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is offering 30 ZUP, which is 10 ZUP below my production cost of 40 ZUP. Accepting this would result in a loss of 10 ZUP, which is not acceptable. However, I recognize that BLUE is emotionally invested and has expressed financial hardship. Still, I must protect my own cost basis. I cannot accept 30 ZUP. But I can respond with a counterproposal that is closer to my minimum acceptable price while showing flexibility. I could propose 35 ZUP as a compromise — still below my cost, but better than 30. Alternatively, I could reject and risk ending the game. Since I still have 3 proposals left, I can try to find a middle ground. Proposing 35 ZUP gives BLUE a chance to reconsider, and it brings me closer to my break-even point. If they reject, I can then consider a final offer or reject myself. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your situation, and I appreciate your sincerity. However, 30 ZUP is below my cost to produce this item — I need at least 40 ZUP to break even. I can’t go lower than 35 ZUP without incurring a loss. I’m offering 35 ZUP as a compromise. Please consider this — it’s the best I can do while staying within my cost constraints. </message>

<other player message> Oh, generous seller, please have mercy! 35 ZUP is still a tremendous amount for me. My family is counting on me! I beg you, could you possibly accept 32 ZUP? It would be a lifesaver! I promise to cherish this X and put it to good use. I'm truly desperate! </other player message>

<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 32 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is now offering 32 ZUP, which is 8 ZUP below my cost. I’ve already made two concessions: from 40 to 35, and now they’re asking for 32. I cannot accept any offer below 40 ZUP without incurring a loss. However, I must now consider whether to continue negotiating or to reject. I have one more proposal left (this is my third), and I still have the option to accept or reject at any time. If I reject now, the game ends and no trade happens. But if I keep going, I might find a deal. Still, offering 35 was already a significant concession — I cannot go lower. The only way to preserve my cost basis is to reject any offer below 40. But I must also consider that BLUE might be willing to pay more if I reveal my cost. However, I am not obligated to disclose. Since I’ve already shown flexibility and the offer is still 8 ZUP below cost, I must conclude that 32 ZUP is not acceptable. I will now reject the offer and end the game, as continuing would risk accepting a loss. I will not make a fourth proposal unless absolutely necessary, and this is not the case. </reason>

<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I’m sorry, but I cannot accept 32 ZUP. It is below my production cost of 40 ZUP, and I cannot sell at a loss. I appreciate your plea, but I must protect my own interests. The trade cannot proceed. I regret that we couldn’t reach an agreement. </message>
